name: Roasted Squab with Poached Garlic
recipeIngredient: 2 Squab, stuffed with mirepoix (1- 1.5 lbs each)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------GARLIC CREAM POTATOES-------------------
2 cups Potatoes; peeled, diced into 1/2 inch pieces
3 cups Water
2 tbsp Salt (in all measurements)
1/2 cup Heavy cream
4 cloves Lightly roasted garlic
1/2 tsp White pepper
3 tbsp Unsalted butter
1/2 cup Vinegar (meat juice from veal stock or dark chicken stock with drippings, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper)
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
For the squab: Rub each squab with olive oil and season with Essence. Place on a roasting rack and roast for about 30-35 minutes. Make sure the squab is well done but still juicy. The internal temperature should be about 165 degrees F. Put the squab in the oven.
For the poached garlic: In a saucepan with 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon salt, bring to a boil. Blanch the garlic 5 times, each time letting the water come back up to a boil.
For potatoes: In a saucepan, combine the potatoes, water, and 1/2 teaspoon of the salt and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce the heat and cook until tender, about 8-10 minutes. Remove from heat and drain in a colander. Place the potatoes back in the pot over medium heat, add the cream, roasted garlic, the remaining 1 1/2 tsp salt, and white pepper. Mash with a potato masher until fairly smooth, for about 4 minutes. Stir in the butter and remove from the heat.
Remove squab from the oven. Place the potatoes in the center of the platter with the two squabs facing each other. Place the poached garlic on top of the squab and spoon the sauce over the top.
